ops.title: cookiesync bridge deployed live at 0.14.2 (core verified); two open follow-ups (Google cookie fidelity, Automation TCC reset) and the agent-browser attach gotcha
ops.body:
The live CDP bridge is **deployed and core-verified on this Mac at cookiesync 0.14.2** (2026-07-16). Verified live: strict **Touch ID tap** opens the bridge; **no raw CDP port** (only the token-gated loopback relay listens; Chrome is on `--remote-debugging-pipe`); seed works; a CDP client **attaches to the seeded OTR context** (Google auth cookies confirmed present); **teardown on disconnect** kills the browser.

**Release history that turn:** cut 0.14.0 (whole bridge), then a **concurrent session** cut 0.14.1 (login-clobber fix: monotone `last_update_utc` writes + syncable-only sync) mid-deploy, which `brew upgrade`'d over my dev-binary symlink. User then forbade dev installs â€” **always cut a real release**, never symlink a `/tmp` dev binary. My seed fix shipped as **0.14.2** (= 0.14.1 + the fix; bridge-only, helper stayed 0.14.0). See [[cookiesync-cdp-bridge-shipped]].

**agent-browser attach gotcha (load-bearing):** attach to a bridge with `AGENT_BROWSER_CDP=<full tokened ws url> agent-browser --session <name> â€¦` (env, persistent session). NEVER `agent-browser connect <loopback url>` â€” on a loopback URL it **relaunches its own cookieless browser** (Gmail shows signin), and the fumbled connect **consumes/kills the single-client bridge slot** (bridge dies). `url` is not a bare command; use `eval 'location.href'`.

**FOLLOW-UP 1 â€” Google session cookie fidelity (do NOT fix inline; user deferred).** Gmail (Profile 3) seeded into the throwaway Chrome lands on `accounts.google.com/.../accountchooser`, not the inbox. A raw-CDP `Storage.getCookies` probe confirmed the **core session cookies DO seed correctly** on `.google.com`/`accounts.google.com` with right attrs+lengths: `__Secure-1PSID`, `__Secure-3PSID`, `__Secure-1PSIDTS`/`3PSIDTS`, `LSID`, `__Host-1PLSID`/`3PLSID`. So it's **not** a seed drop of the session cookies â€” most likely Google's **device-bound re-auth** (fresh browser fingerprint) is challenging, a genuine seeded-clone fidelity limit. Proper follow-up: a **domain-filtered** cookie probe (my first probe matched cookie names across all 4770 cookies, so `SID`/`SAPISID` collided with `.united.com`/`.google.ca` â€” inconclusive on HSID/APISID), check the bridge open `skipped`/undecryptable-v20 count, and confirm device-binding vs a subtle cookie gap. Cookie-only logins on less-paranoid sites are expected to work.

**FOLLOW-UP 2 â€” Automation TCC permission resets every cask update.** The headed bridge Chrome needs the "modify other apps" (AppleEvents/Automation) TCC grant; every `brew upgrade` of cookiesync changes the binary path â†’ macOS re-prompts and the grant is lost. User re-granted it 3+ times this session and wants it **stabilized** (stable signing identity / TCC handling that survives updates). Needs its own plan.

**Still-pending live verifies:** `ab --bridge`, `agent-browser --provider cookiesync`, and 2-host Phase B (yasyf-home needs `brew upgrade` to 0.14.2 too).
